findings.evidence: grep Mathlib for `^def mapAlgHom` and `AlgHom.compLeft` shows `map*` is the established convention for `φ ↦ postcomp φ`; `AlgHom.mapValue` does not appear in Mathlib outside the unrelated `ValuativeExtension.mapValueGroupWithZero`.
findings.fix: Rename AlgHom.mapValue to AlgHom.map (or AlgHom.compMonoidHom / AlgHom.postcomp), and rename mapValue_apply, mapValue_id, mapValue_comp accordingly; update the module docstring bullet for the same.
findings.issue: AlgHom.mapValue uses the category-theoretic term 'value' (a functor evaluated at an object) for what is just post-composition of an AlgHom lifted to a MonoidHom of convolution monoids. The only existing mapValue* declaration in Mathlib is ValuativeExtension.mapValueGroupWithZero; the standard naming for φ : A →ₐ[R] B ↦ (postcomp φ : X →ₐ[R] A →* X →ₐ[R] B) is the map family (cf. MvPolynomial.mapAlgHom, PowerSeries.mapAlgHom, AdjoinRoot.mapAlgHom) or postcomp/comp.

summary: Names are mostly consistent with Mathlib's Convolution.lean (e.g. convInv_apply mirrors convMul_apply/convOne_apply, instGroup/instCommGroup follow the instance-naming convention), but mapValue introduces category-theoretic terminology that has no precedent in the surrounding AlgHom/WithConv/MonoidHom context.
